about.title: What is Eid?
about.subtitle: Understanding the Blessings of Islamic Festivals
about.content_p1: The word 'Eid' means a festival or a celebration that returns every year. In Islam, there are two major festivals: Eid-ul-Fitr and Eid-ul-Adha.
about.content_p2: Eid is a time of joy, gratitude, and worship. It is a day when Muslims worldwide gather to thank Allah for His countless blessings and mercy.
about.fitr_desc: Celebrated at the end of Ramadan, marking the completion of the month of fasting. It is a day of giving thanks to Allah for the strength to fast.
about.adha_desc: The Festival of Sacrifice, celebrated during the month of Dhul-Hijjah to commemorate Prophet Ibrahim's willingness to sacrifice his son in obedience to Allah.

sunnah.title: Sunnah of Eid
sunnah.subtitle: Practices of the Prophet (PBUH) on Eid Day

prayer.title: Eid Prayer Guide
prayer.subtitle: How to Perform the Eid Salah Step-by-Step

sadaqah.title: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr
sadaqah.subtitle: Obligatory Charity of Fasting
sadaqah.content_p1: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr (or Zakat-ul-Fitr) is an obligatory charity that every Muslim must pay before the Eid-ul-Fitr prayer.

dos.item1: Show joy and happiness to others.
dos.item2: Dress neatly and cleanly, preferably in new or best clothes.
dos.item3: Visit family, relatives, and friends to strengthen ties of kinship.
dos.item5: Remember the poor and needy by giving charity and sharing food.
dos.item6: Forgive those who have wronged you and reconcile broken relationships.
dos.item7: Praise Allah abundantly throughout the day by reciting Takbeerat.

donts.item1: Fasting on the day of Eid (it is historically forbidden).
donts.item2: Missing the compulsory daily prayers (Fajr, Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib, Isha) due to celebrations.
donts.item3: Engaging in extravagance, excessive spending, or showing off in dress and food.
donts.item4: Mixing freely in a way that violates Islamic modesty guidelines.
donts.item5: Listening to impermissible music or engaging in un-Islamic entertainment.
donts.item6: Forgetting Allah's remembrance amidst the festivities.
donts.item7: Holding grudges and refusing to make peace with others.

revamped_home.hero_subtitle: Master every detail of Eid celebration from the spirit of the day to the prayer method and Sadaqah rules.
revamped_home.chapter1_desc: Explore the meaning, blessings, and initial Sunnahs that set the tone for this holy celebration.
revamped_home.chapter2_desc: A complete guide to the Takbeerat, method, and congregation rules for the Eid Salah.
revamped_home.chapter3_desc: Learn about the calculation, eligibility, and importance of the charity given before Eid prayer.
revamped_home.chapter4_desc: Discover the recommended acts, things to avoid, and the deeper wisdom behind this joyous day.

share_cards.greeting_content: May Allah bless you and your family with boundless joy and prosperity on this holy occasion.
share_cards.sunnah_content: Ghusl, Good Clothes, Dates, Sadaqah, Takbeerat, and Eid Prayer.
share_cards.takbeer_content: Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar, La ilaha illallah, Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar, wa lillahil Hamd
share_cards.charity_content: Don't forget to give your Fitrana before the Eid prayer to help those in need.

page0.title: The Night of Eid (Chand Raat) and Special Devotion
page0.subtitle: Chand Raat Action Plan
page0.intro_message: Chand Raat is actually the night to receive our “Eidi” (reward) from Allah. Throughout Ramadan we fasted, prayed Taraweeh, gave Zakat, gave charity, and recited the Qur’an. Now this is the night when we hope that Allah accepts all our acts of worship and grants us their reward. Therefore, instead of spending this night in heedlessness, we should increase our worship.
page0.q1_question: Question: Is there any special virtue in performing acts of worship on the night of the moon sighting (Chand Raat)?
page0.q1_answer: Answer: Yes, certainly. There is a reported special virtue for standing in worship on this night. Consider the following hadith.
page0.hadith_intro: It is narrated from Hazrat Abu Umamah (RA) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
page0.hadith_arabic: "من قام ليلتي العيدين محتسباً لله لم يمت قلبه يوم تموت القلوب۔"
page0.hadith_translation: Whoever stands in worship on the nights of the two Eids, seeking reward from Allah, his heart will not die on the day when hearts will die.

actions.item1: Make dua for protection of faith.
actions.item5: Engage in remembrance of Allah and send blessings upon Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
actions.item6: Ask for love of the Prophet ﷺ.
actions.item8: Seek protection from punishment of the grave and Hellfire.
actions.item10: Ask forgiveness from parents and relatives.

page0.note: Note: Although this hadith is considered weak, scholars of hadith allow mentioning it in matters related to virtues of deeds. Many Muslims become negligent after the 27th night of Ramadan and waste valuable time. Therefore, special effort should also be made to worship on the night of Eid.

page1.title: Explanation of Eid-ul-Fitr
page1.p1: In Islamic law, several days are designated for expressing happiness and gratitude. Among these days is the one that comes at the end of the blessed month of Ramadan, which is called Eid-ul-Fitr. On this day Muslims thank Allah Almighty for granting them the ability to worship during Ramadan.
page1.p2: On the day of Eid-ul-Fitr, Muslims perform the Eid prayer, give Sadaqat-ul-Fitr, and express joy. It is recommended to wear good clothes, apply perfume, and exchange greetings.
page1.p3: Muslims should thank Allah on this day and strive further in obedience to Him. They should also care for the poor and needy so that they can also share in the happiness.
page1.p4: Therefore, the virtues and rulings related to Eid-ul-Fitr are explained below.

page2.title: Definition of Eid
page2.subtitle: Basic acts and meaning of Eid

list_items.item2: Celebrating the joy of Eid is a Sunnah

page2.question: Question: What is meant by Eid?
page2.answer: Answer: Eid means the return of happiness. It refers to a day when people express joy and gratitude for the blessings of Allah.
page2.quran_text: In the Qur'an it is mentioned:
page2.quran_quote: “And do not be excessive. Indeed, He does not like those who commit excess.”
page2.hadith_text: In a Hadith, the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ said:
page2.hadith_quote: “Every nation has its festival, and this is our festival.”

page3.title: Special Practices on Eid Day
page3.p1: Some acts of worship and special practices on the day of Eid
page3.q1_question: Question: Besides acts of worship, what other things should be observed on the day of Eid?
page3.q1_answer: Answer: Apart from worship, the following practices should be observed on the day of Eid.
page3.hadith_intro: Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
page3.hadith_arabic: "من قام لیلتی العیدین محتسباً لم یمت قلبہ یوم تموت القلوب"
page3.hadith_translation: “Whoever stands in worship on the nights of the two Eids seeking reward, his heart will not die on the day when hearts die.”
page3.note: Note: If possible, one should spend the night of Eid in worship to gain the special mercy of Allah.
page3.q2_title: Supplications on the day of Eid
page3.q2_question: Question: What should be recited on the day of Eid?
page3.q2_answer: Answer: On the day of Eid, one should recite Takbeerat and other remembrances (Azkar) and praise Allah.

page4.title: Sunnah of Eid Morning
page4.p1: On the day of Eid, it is Sunnah to wake up early, take a bath, wear good clothes, and apply perfume. On Eid-ul-Fitr, it is recommended to eat something sweet (such as dates) before going for the Eid prayer.
page4.p2: The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ used to eat a few dates before going to the Eid prayer.
page4.h1_intro:
A Hadith states:

Anas bin Malik (RA) reported:
page4.h1_quote: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ would not go out on the morning of Eid-ul-Fitr until he had eaten some dates.”
page4.h2_intro: Another narration from Abu Saeed Al-Khudri (RA) states:
page4.h2_quote: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ would not perform any prayer before the Eid prayer.”

page5.title: Fasting and Recommended Acts
page5.q1_question: Question: Is it permissible to fast on the day of Eid?
page5.q1_answer: Answer: No. It is forbidden to fast on the days of Eid-ul-Fitr and Eid-ul-Adha.
page5.hadith_arabic: نَهَى النَّبِيُّ ﷺ عَنْ صَوْمِ يَوْمِ الْفِطْرِ وَيَوْمِ النَّحْرِ۔
page5.hadith_quote: “The Prophet ﷺ forbade fasting on the day of Eid-ul-Fitr and the day of Eid-ul-Adha.”
page5.p2: Sunnahs and Recommended Acts on Eid-ul-Fitr
page5.q2_question: Question: What Sunnahs and recommended acts should be observed on Eid-ul-Fitr?
page5.q2_answer: Answer: The following Sunnahs should be observed:

page6.title: Etiquettes of the Day of Eid
page6.subtitle: Etiquettes that should be observed
page6.q_question: Question: What etiquettes should be observed on the day of Eid?
page6.q_answer: Answer: The following etiquettes should be observed on the day of Eid:

list_items.item1: Wake up early in the morning

page6.hadith_quote1: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to go to the Eid prayer ground on foot on the day of Eid.
page6.hadith_quote2: And he used to return by a different route.

page7.title: Greetings and Happiness
page7.q1_question: Question: Is it correct to greet each other on the day of Eid?
page7.q1_answer: Answer: Yes, it is permissible to greet each other on the day of Eid.
page7.hadith_intro: The Companions (RA) used to say to each other on Eid:
page7.hadith_translation: Meaning: May Allah accept (good deeds) from us and from you.
page7.scholar_note: Islamic scholars have also considered exchanging greetings on Eid to be permissible.
page7.p2_desc1: On the day of Eid, one should express happiness and treat their family members kindly.
page7.p2_desc2: However, it should be ensured that the expression of happiness does not involve any sinful or inappropriate actions.

page8.title: Ruling and Time of Eid Prayer
page8.q1_question: Question: What is the ruling of the Eid prayer?
page8.q1_answer: Answer: The Eid prayer is obligatory (Wajib).
page8.p1_desc: According to the Hanafi scholars, the prayers of Eid-ul-Fitr and Eid-ul-Adha are obligatory.
page8.hadith_intro: Hazrat Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA) narrated that the Prophet ﷺ said:
page8.hadith_quote: “The prayer of Eid-ul-Fitr and Eid-ul-Adha is obligatory upon Muslims.”
page8.hadith_desc: The Eid prayer is an important act of worship in which Muslims gather together.
page8.q2_question: Question: When does the time of Eid prayer begin?
page8.q2_answer: Answer: The time of Eid prayer begins after sunrise and continues until before noon (Zawal).

page9.title: Rak‘ahs and Takbeers
page9.q1_question: Question: How many Rak‘ahs are there in the Eid prayer?
page9.q1_answer: Answer: The Eid prayer consists of two Rak‘ahs.
page9.p1_desc: This prayer is performed in congregation, and it includes additional Takbeers.
page9.hadith_quote: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to perform two Rak‘ahs for the prayers of Eid-ul-Fitr and Eid-ul-Adha.
page9.q2_question: Question: How many Takbeers are there in the Eid prayer?
page9.q2_answer: Answer: There are six additional Takbeers in the Eid prayer.
page9.p2_desc1: Three Takbeers in the first Rak‘ah
page9.p2_desc2: Three Takbeers in the second Rak‘ah

page10.title: Method of Performing Eid Prayer
page10.subtitle: Method of Performing Eid Prayer
page10.q_question: Question: How is the Eid prayer performed?
page10.q_answer: Answer: The Eid prayer consists of two Rak‘ahs and is performed in congregation.
page10.rakah1: In the first Rak‘ah, the Imam says the opening Takbeer (Takbeer-e-Tahrima) and folds his hands, then recites the Sana. After that, three additional Takbeers are said, raising the hands with each Takbeer.
page10.rakah1_cont: Then the Imam recites Surah Al-Fatiha and another Surah, followed by Ruku and Sujood, completing the first Rak‘ah.
page10.rakah2: In the second Rak‘ah, after reciting Surah Al-Fatiha and another Surah, three additional Takbeers are said. Then Ruku is performed, and the prayer is completed.

page11.title: Khutbah after Eid Prayer
page11.q1_question: Question: Is a sermon delivered after the Eid prayer?
page11.q1_answer: Answer: Yes, a sermon (Khutbah) is delivered after the Eid prayer.
page11.p1_desc: The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ used to deliver a Khutbah after the Eid prayer and advise the people.
page11.hadith_quote: On the day of Eid, after performing the prayer, the Prophet ﷺ stood and delivered a sermon to the people.
page11.p2_desc: Listening to the Khutbah is Sunnah, and Muslims should listen to it quietly and attentively.

page12.title: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr (Charity of Fitr)
page12.q1_answer: Answer: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr is a charity that becomes obligatory on every eligible Muslim (Sahib-e-Nisab) at the end of the month of Ramadan.
page12.p1_desc: The purpose of giving Sadaqat-ul-Fitr is that poor and needy people can also share in the happiness of Eid.
page12.hadith_quote: The Prophet ﷺ made Sadaqat-ul-Fitr obligatory.
page12.p2_desc: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr should be given before the Eid prayer.

page13.title: Who is obligated to give Sadaqat-ul-Fitr
page13.p1: Who is obligated to give Sadaqat-ul-Fitr
page13.q1_question: Question: On whom is Sadaqat-ul-Fitr obligatory?
page13.q1_answer: Answer: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr is obligatory on every Muslim who possesses Nisab (minimum required wealth).
page13.p1_desc1: This means a person who has wealth beyond their basic needs.
page13.p1_desc2: Every person who meets the Nisab requirement must give Sadaqat-ul-Fitr on their own behalf and on behalf of their minor children.
page13.hadith_intro: Hazrat Abdullah ibn Umar (RA) narrated that the Prophet ﷺ said:

page14.title: Amount of Sadaqat-ul-Fitr
page14.q1_question: Question: What is the amount of Sadaqat-ul-Fitr?
page14.q1_answer: Answer: The amount of Sadaqat-ul-Fitr is one Saa'.
page14.p1_desc1: One Saa' is approximately equal to four Mud.
page14.p1_desc2: This amount can be given in the form of wheat, barley, dates, or raisins.
page14.hadith_quote: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prescribed one Saa' of dates or one Saa' of barley as Sadaqat-ul-Fitr.

page15.title: Who should receive Sadaqat-ul-Fitr
page15.q1_question: Question: To whom can Sadaqat-ul-Fitr be given?
page15.q1_answer: Answer: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr can be given to those who are eligible for Zakat.
page15.p1_desc: This includes the poor, the needy, and those in financial hardship.
page15.quran_translation: “Indeed, charities are for the poor and the needy.”
page15.p2_desc: Therefore, Sadaqat-ul-Fitr should be given to people who are truly in need.

page16.title: When should Sadaqat-ul-Fitr be given
page16.q1_question: Question: When should Sadaqat-ul-Fitr be given?
page16.q1_answer: Answer: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr should be given before the Eid-ul-Fitr prayer.
page16.p1_desc: If someone gives Sadaqat-ul-Fitr after the Eid prayer, the charity will still count as a normal charity but it will not carry the reward of Sadaqat-ul-Fitr.
page16.hadith_intro: Hazrat Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA) narrated that the Prophet ﷺ said:
page16.hadith_quote: “Whoever gives Sadaqat-ul-Fitr before the Eid prayer, it is accepted as Fitr charity, and whoever gives it after the prayer, it is just a charity among other charities.”

page17.title: Benefits of Sadaqat-ul-Fitr
page17.q1_question: Question: What are the benefits of giving Sadaqat-ul-Fitr?
page17.q1_answer: Answer: There are several benefits of giving Sadaqat-ul-Fitr:
page17.p1_desc1: It purifies the fasting person from mistakes and shortcomings in their fasting.
page17.p1_desc2: It helps the poor and needy.
page17.p1_desc3: It allows poor people to share in the happiness of Eid.
page17.hadith_intro: Hazrat Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA) narrated that the Prophet ﷺ said:
page17.hadith_quote: “Sadaqat-ul-Fitr is a means of purification for the fasting person from idle talk and sins, and it is food for the needy.”

page18.title: Recommended Acts on the Day of Eid
page18.p1: Recommended Acts on the Day of Eid
page18.q1_question: Question: What acts are recommended on the day of Eid?
page18.q1_answer: Answer: The following acts are recommended on the day of Eid:
page18.li5: Going to the Eid prayer ground on foot
page18.hadith_quote: Taking a bath on the day of Eid is recommended (Mustahab).

page19.title: Things to Avoid on the Day of Eid
page19.p1: Things to Avoid on the Day of Eid
page19.q1_question: Question: What things should be avoided on the day of Eid?
page19.q1_answer: Answer: The following things should be avoided on the day of Eid:
page19.p2_desc: Muslims should celebrate Eid with happiness but must ensure that they do not perform any act that goes against Islamic teachings.

page20.title: Wisdom Behind Eid
page20.q1_question: Question: What is the wisdom behind celebrating Eid?
page20.q1_answer: Answer: The wisdom behind celebrating Eid is that Muslims express joy for the blessings of Allah and show gratitude to Him.
page20.p1_desc1: After observing the fasts and performing worship during the month of Ramadan, Allah has granted Muslims the happiness of Eid.
page20.p1_desc2: Eid also serves as a means of creating love, brotherhood, and unity among Muslims.
page20.p1_desc3: On this day, Muslims meet one another, exchange greetings, and express happiness.

page21.title: Summary
page21.p1_desc1: Eid-ul-Fitr is a day of joy and gratitude for Muslims. On this day, Muslims thank Allah for granting them the ability to observe the fasts and perform acts of worship during the blessed month of Ramadan.
page21.p1_desc2: On Eid, Muslims perform the Eid prayer, give Sadaqat-ul-Fitr, and express love and brotherhood among themselves.
page21.p1_desc3: Islam teaches us that we should also include the poor and needy in our happiness.
page21.p1_desc4: Therefore, on the day of Eid we should thank Allah, perform good deeds, and treat others kindly.

eid_dua.translation: May Allah accept (good deeds) from us and from you.
eid_dua.context: When the companions of the Prophet (PBUH) met on the day of Eid, they would say this to each other as a beautiful prayer of acceptance.
